description: Rate at which the controller server computes velocity commands.
impact: Higher values give smoother control but increase CPU load. Lower values may cause jerky motion or tracking lag.

description: Minimum absolute x velocity for the robot to be considered moving.
impact: Used to detect if the robot is stuck. Too high and slight drift triggers stuck detection; too low and a truly stuck robot is not detected.

description: Minimum absolute y velocity (for holonomic robots) to be considered moving.
impact: Only relevant for holonomic platforms. Ignored for differential-drive robots.

description: Minimum absolute angular velocity to be considered rotating.
impact: Used in stuck detection. Robots that spin slowly in place may falsely appear stuck if set too high.

description: Duration the controller is allowed to fail before the action server returns failure.
impact: Longer tolerance allows temporary obstacles to clear. Too long and the robot remains stuck waiting.

description: Plugin name for the progress checker that detects if the robot is making progress.
impact: Changing requires a node restart. The progress checker determines when to declare the robot stuck.

description: List of goal checker plugin names used to determine when the goal is reached.
impact: Requires node restart. Multiple goal checkers can be chained; only one needs to succeed.

description: List of controller plugin names loaded by the controller server.
impact: Requires node restart. Each name maps to a plugin type declared in the YAML.

description: C++ plugin class for the FollowPath controller action.
impact: Requires node restart. Switching between DWB, RPP, and MPPI changes the entire control strategy.

description: Target forward speed for the RegulatedPurePursuit controller.
impact: Higher speeds reduce traversal time but decrease safety margins and path tracking accuracy.

description: Fixed lookahead distance for RPP when adaptive lookahead is disabled.
impact: Larger values produce smoother paths but may cut corners. Smaller values track the path more precisely but can cause oscillation.

description: Minimum lookahead distance when adaptive lookahead is enabled.
impact: Prevents the lookahead point from being so close that the robot oscillates.

description: Maximum lookahead distance when adaptive lookahead is enabled.
impact: Caps how far ahead the robot looks. Too large and it may leave the path in narrow spaces.

description: Time horizon used to compute adaptive lookahead distance (dist = speed * time).
impact: Longer time makes the robot look further ahead at higher speeds, adapting to velocity.

description: Scale the lookahead distance proportionally to the robot's current speed.
impact: Enables adaptive lookahead. At low speeds, the robot looks close; at high speeds, it looks far ahead.

description: Minimum speed when approaching the goal to prevent stopping before reaching it.
impact: Too low and the robot creeps at the goal; too high and it overshoots.

description: Distance from the goal at which the robot begins slowing down.
impact: Larger values cause earlier deceleration. Tune based on robot mass and max speed.

description: Reduce linear velocity when the path curvature is high (tight turns).
impact: Prevents the robot from taking corners too fast and leaving the path.

description: Reduce linear velocity when the robot is near obstacles (high costmap cost).
impact: Adds a safety margin near obstacles. Disable in open environments to improve speed.

description: Path curvature radius below which linear velocity scaling activates.
impact: Smaller values only slow down for very tight turns; larger values slow for moderate curves too.

description: Minimum speed the regulated velocity scaling will reduce to (floor).
impact: Prevents the robot from stopping completely when regulated. Tune with approach velocity.

description: Maximum angular acceleration applied during heading correction.
impact: Limits how quickly the robot spins. Low values give smooth turns; high values allow fast corrections but may tip cargo.

description: Rotate in place to face the path direction before moving forward.
impact: Improves path tracking at the start but slows departure. Disable for holonomic robots or when in-place rotation is unsafe.

description: Minimum heading error before the rotate-to-heading behavior activates.
impact: ~45° default. Smaller values cause frequent in-place rotations; larger values allow more heading error before correcting.

description: Angular velocity used during in-place rotate-to-heading maneuvers.
impact: Higher values rotate faster but may overshoot. Lower values are smoother but slower.

description: Allow the RPP controller to drive the robot in reverse.
impact: Required for backing-into-dock or reverse navigation. Can cause unexpected behavior if the planner does not account for it.

description: List of planner plugin names loaded by the planner server.
impact: Requires node restart. Each entry must have a corresponding plugin type in the YAML.

description: Expected rate at which planning requests will arrive. Used for timeout warning.
impact: Does not directly control the planning rate. A mismatch logs warnings.

description: C++ plugin class for the GridBased path planner.
impact: Requires node restart. NavFn is fast but non-kinematically-feasible; SmacHybrid produces car-like paths; ThetaStar uses any-angle paths.

description: Distance tolerance from the goal for NavFn/Theta* planners to consider planning successful.
impact: Larger tolerance allows planning to approximate goals in cluttered areas. Smaller values require an exact path to the goal.

description: Use A* instead of Dijkstra's algorithm in NavFn. A* is faster but less optimal in costmaps with large cost gradients.
impact: A* is faster for long paths; Dijkstra finds the globally optimal path in complex cost fields.

description: Allow the planner to plan through unknown (unmapped) space.
impact: True enables planning in partially explored maps. False requires a complete map before navigating.

description: Maximum number of SmacPlanner iterations before declaring planning failed.
impact: Prevents infinite planning loops. Set lower in time-critical applications; higher for large maps.

description: Minimum turning radius for the SmacPlannerHybrid (Ackermann/car-like constraint).
impact: Must match the physical robot. Too small: the plan is kinematically infeasible. Too large: the robot cannot navigate tight spaces.

description: Number of discrete heading angles in the SmacPlannerHybrid search space.
impact: More bins give finer heading resolution but increase memory and planning time. 72 bins = 5° resolution.

description: Apply path smoothing after planning to reduce sharp corners.
impact: Produces more natural paths. May slightly increase planning time. Disable for pure waypoint following.

description: Minimum number of particles maintained by the AMCL particle filter.
impact: Fewer particles reduce CPU usage but decrease localization accuracy. Critical in ambiguous environments.

description: Maximum number of particles allowed. The filter scales up to this during high uncertainty.
impact: Higher values improve localization during recovery but increase CPU and memory. Tune with min_particles.

description: Sensor model used to weight particles against laser scan data.
impact: likelihood_field is fast and works well for most 2D lidars. beam is more accurate for ray-traced maps but slower.

description: Maximum valid laser range. Readings beyond this are discarded.
impact: Set to the lidar's spec max. Ignoring distant returns reduces noise from reflections off glass or mirrors.

description: Minimum valid laser range. -1 uses the lidar's own minimum. Readings closer than this are discarded.
impact: Eliminates returns from the robot's own body. Set to the clearance radius of the sensor mount.

description: Number of evenly-spaced laser beams used per update. Downsamples the full scan.
impact: Fewer beams reduce computation. More beams improve accuracy in geometrically complex environments.

description: Likelihood field model: weight for hits (beam lands on occupied map cell).
impact: Together with z_rand, z_max, z_short these must sum to 1.0. Higher z_hit makes the filter trust map matches more.

description: Likelihood field model: weight for random/uniform returns (glass, mirrors, dynamic objects).
impact: Increase in noisy or dynamic environments. Reduces sensitivity to outlier laser returns.

description: Standard deviation of the Gaussian used for hit probability in the likelihood field model.
impact: Larger values widen the acceptance window around map obstacles. Tune to your lidar's measurement noise.

description: Motion model plugin type: differential for diff-drive, omnidirectional for holonomic robots.
impact: Must match the robot's drive type. Using differential on an omni robot will produce poor localization.

description: Odometry noise: rotation error from rotation.
impact: Increase if the robot's heading drifts during pure rotation (e.g., slipping wheels).

description: Odometry noise: rotation error from translation.
impact: Increase if the robot's heading drifts while driving straight.

description: Odometry noise: translation error from translation.
impact: Increase for robots with high wheel slip. Affects how spread the particle cloud becomes when driving.

description: Odometry noise: translation error from rotation.
impact: Increase if the robot moves slightly forward when commanded to rotate in place.

description: Minimum translation the robot must travel before an AMCL update is triggered.
impact: Smaller values update more frequently (better accuracy, more CPU). Larger values reduce CPU but may allow localization to lag.

description: Minimum rotation the robot must perform before an AMCL update is triggered.
impact: Smaller values trigger updates for tiny rotations. Tune with update_min_d to avoid redundant updates when stationary.

description: Number of filter updates between particle resampling steps.
impact: Higher values reduce particle deprivation by allowing more observation updates before resampling.

description: Exponential decay rate for the slow average weight filter used in global localization recovery.
impact: 0.0 disables recovery. Non-zero enables injecting random particles when the filter diverges (kidnapped robot).

description: Exponential decay rate for the fast average weight filter used in global localization recovery.
impact: Used with recovery_alpha_slow. When fast/slow weight ratio drops, random particles are injected.

description: The coordinate frame in which AMCL publishes the pose estimate.
impact: Must match the TF tree. Changing requires a node restart.

description: The odometry frame used by AMCL to compute the map→odom correction.
impact: Must match the TF tree published by your odometry source. Changing requires a node restart.

description: The robot's base frame. AMCL computes the pose of this frame in the map.
impact: Must match the robot's URDF base link. Changing requires a node restart.

description: Whether AMCL broadcasts the map→odom TF transform.
impact: Disable if another node (e.g., a GPS fuser) provides this transform. Having two sources causes TF conflicts.

description: Tick rate of the Behavior Tree main loop in milliseconds.
impact: Lower values (faster tick) improve responsiveness but increase CPU. 10ms (100Hz) is standard.

description: Default timeout for BT action server calls that do not specify their own timeout.
impact: Prevents the BT from hanging indefinitely if a server does not respond. Tune per deployment complexity.

description: How long to wait for a ROS2 service to become available before timing out.
impact: Increase in slow-startup environments (arm-embedded hardware). Lower for fast-boot systems.

description: Global coordinate frame used by the BT navigator for goals and poses.
impact: Must match the map frame. Changing requires a node restart.

description: Robot base frame used by the BT navigator.
impact: Must match the URDF base link. Changing requires a node restart.

description: Enable ZMQ publisher for Groot2 BT visualization and debugging.
impact: Adds a ZMQ dependency and slight overhead. Only enable when debugging BT behavior.

description: Rate at which the local costmap updates from sensor data.
impact: Higher values react faster to new obstacles but increase CPU. Must be at or below the sensor publish rate.

description: Rate at which the local costmap is published for visualization (RViz, Foxglove).
impact: Does not affect navigation performance. Lower values reduce bandwidth.

description: Reference frame for the local costmap. Typically 'odom' for local planning.
impact: Using odom means the costmap moves with the robot. Requires TF from this frame to base_link.

description: Robot base frame used to center the rolling local costmap.
impact: Must match the URDF base link. Changing requires a costmap restart.

description: Keep the costmap centered on the robot (rolling window) rather than fixed in the world.
impact: Required for local costmaps. Enabling on global costmap would be wrong.

description: Width of the local costmap in meters.
impact: Larger maps give the controller more obstacle context but use more memory and computation.

description: Height of the local costmap in meters.
impact: Larger maps give the controller more obstacle context but use more memory and computation.

description: Cell size of the local costmap grid.
impact: Finer resolution (smaller value) captures narrow obstacles but increases computation. Should match the lidar's angular resolution.

description: Polygon footprint of the robot in base_link frame for collision checking.
impact: Must accurately represent the robot's physical extent including payload. Too small risks collisions; too large restricts navigation in tight spaces.

description: Circular approximation of the robot radius for costmap collision checks (used when footprint is not set).
impact: Set to the robot's actual radius including payload. Larger values add safety margin but restrict narrow passage navigation.

description: Ordered list of costmap layer plugins. Layers are stacked in order.
impact: Requires costmap restart. The order matters: obstacle layers run before inflation so inflation sees obstacles.

description: Radius around obstacles that gets inflated with a cost gradient.
impact: Key safety parameter. Must be >= robot_radius + clearance. Too small causes collisions; too large blocks narrow corridors.

description: Exponential decay rate for the inflation cost gradient away from obstacles.
impact: Higher values create steeper drop-off (robot prefers paths further from obstacles). Lower values create a gentler gradient.

description: Enable or disable the 2D obstacle layer.
impact: Disabling removes 2D lidar obstacle detection. Only disable if using voxel_layer exclusively.

description: Maximum range at which new obstacles are inserted into the obstacle layer.
impact: Larger values populate the costmap with distant obstacles. Too large: cluttered costmap from far noise. Too small: late obstacle detection.

description: Minimum range for obstacle insertion. Readings closer than this are ignored.
impact: Filters out returns from the robot body. Set equal to the lidar mount clearance from the robot's edge.

description: Maximum range for ray-tracing free space through the obstacle layer.
impact: Longer ray traces clear more space in front of the robot as it moves, removing stale obstacles faster.

description: Enable or disable the 3D voxel obstacle layer (requires 3D sensor input).
impact: Needed for 3D sensors (RGB-D, 3D lidar). Disable on 2D-lidar-only platforms to save memory.

description: Number of vertical voxel layers in the 3D voxel grid.
impact: More layers capture taller obstacles (overhead beams) but increase memory. Tune to the sensor's vertical FOV.

description: Height of each voxel in the z dimension.
impact: Smaller values detect thinner horizontal obstacles (low beams, thresholds) but increase computation.

description: Rate at which the global costmap updates. Usually slower than local since it covers the whole map.
impact: Lower values reduce CPU for large maps. Must be at least as fast as the planning rate.

description: Rate at which the global costmap is published for visualization.
impact: Lower values reduce bandwidth on large maps. Does not affect planning.

description: Reference frame for the global costmap. Must match the map server's frame.
impact: Requires costmap restart. Must be 'map' in typical Nav2 deployments.

description: Robot base frame used by the global costmap for footprint placement.
impact: Must match the URDF base link. Changing requires a costmap restart.

description: Cell size of the global costmap grid. Should match or be a multiple of the map server resolution.
impact: Finer resolution enables more precise path planning but increases memory. For large environments, 0.1m may be appropriate.

description: Ordered list of global costmap layer plugins.
impact: Requires costmap restart. static_layer provides the base map; obstacle_layer adds dynamic obstacles; inflation_layer expands them.

description: Enable the static map layer that loads the occupancy grid from the map server.
impact: Disabling removes the pre-built map from planning. Only disable for pure sensor-based navigation.

description: Subscribe to the map topic with transient local QoS (receive last published map even if late).
impact: Required when the map is published before Nav2 starts. Disable if using a live map that updates frequently.

description: Radius around obstacles inflated in the global costmap.
impact: Key safety parameter for path planning. Controls how close to walls and obstacles the global path can go.

description: Exponential cost decay away from obstacles in the global costmap inflation layer.
impact: Higher values keep the planner further from walls. Must be consistent with the local costmap for smooth handoff.

description: List of path smoother plugin names.
impact: Requires node restart. SimpleSmoother applies iterative averaging; ConstrainedSmoother uses optimization.

description: Convergence tolerance for the simple smoother. Smaller values run more iterations.
impact: Very small values produce smoother paths but increase CPU. The default is already very tight.

description: Maximum smoothing iterations per path.
impact: Caps computation time. Paths that don't converge within this limit use the best result so far.

description: Weight for the smoothness cost (pulls waypoints toward their neighbors).
impact: Higher values produce smoother but shorter paths that may deviate from the original. Must balance with w_data.

description: Weight for the data (path fidelity) cost — keeps waypoints close to the original path.
impact: Higher values preserve the original path shape more faithfully. Balance with w_smooth.

description: List of recovery behavior plugin names available to the BT navigator.
impact: Requires node restart. Only listed behaviors can be invoked from BTs. Add AssistedTeleop for manual override support.

description: How far ahead in time the spin behavior simulates its rotation to check for collisions.
impact: Longer look-ahead prevents spinning into obstacles but may abort valid recovery spins.

description: How far ahead in time the backup behavior simulates its reverse motion for collision checking.
impact: Prevents backing into unseen obstacles. Too conservative and valid backups are aborted.

description: Global frame used by the behavior server for pose tracking during recovery behaviors.
impact: Must match the costmap global frame for accurate collision simulation.

description: Robot base frame used by the behavior server.
impact: Must match the URDF base link and other Nav2 nodes.

description: Rate at which velocity commands are smoothed and republished.
impact: Higher values produce more responsive output. Must be >= the controller frequency to avoid introducing latency.

description: Scale all velocity components proportionally so none exceed their limits, rather than clamping independently.
impact: True preserves the intended motion direction. False may distort arcs by clamping components separately.

description: Velocity feedback mode. OPEN_LOOP uses commanded velocities; CLOSED_LOOP uses odometry.
impact: CLOSED_LOOP is more accurate for robots with slippage but requires reliable odometry. OPEN_LOOP is simpler and works without odom feedback.

description: Maximum [x, y, theta] velocities. y is non-zero for holonomic robots.
impact: Hard ceiling on commanded velocities. Mismatching this with the robot's actual limits causes clipping.

description: Minimum [x, y, theta] velocities (negative = reverse / opposite direction).
impact: Defines the lower velocity bound. Set min_x > 0 to prevent reversing for non-reversible robots.

description: Maximum [x, y, theta] acceleration limits.
impact: Must match the robot's physical limits. Too high: wheel slip or tip-over. Too low: sluggish response.

description: Maximum [x, y, theta] deceleration limits (negative values).
impact: Controls stopping distance. Conservative (small magnitude) values produce gentle stops. Aggressive values stop fast but may tip cargo.

description: Velocity commands below this threshold are zeroed out (deadband).
impact: Eliminates micro-movements that the robot cannot physically execute. Useful for robots with motor dead zones.

description: Time after which a velocity command is considered stale and zeroed.
impact: Safety feature: robot stops if no new commands arrive. Lower values are safer but may cause stops during momentary message drops.

description: Rate at which the waypoint follower checks for goal completion.
impact: Higher values detect goal completion sooner. Rarely needs tuning.

description: Stop the waypoint sequence if any single waypoint navigation fails.
impact: True is conservative (stops on first failure). False attempts all waypoints even if some fail.

description: Plugin executed at each waypoint after arrival (e.g., wait, take photo, dock).
impact: Requires node restart. Custom plugins enable task execution at each stop.

description: Time to pause at each waypoint before proceeding to the next, in milliseconds.
impact: Non-zero enables dwell time at waypoints. Useful for loading/unloading stations.

description: Path to the map YAML file describing the occupancy grid.
impact: Changing this path reloads the map immediately via the load_map service — no restart needed.

description: Topic name on which the map server publishes the occupancy grid.
impact: Must match the topic subscribed by AMCL, global costmap static layer, and RViz.

description: TF frame ID embedded in the published map message.
impact: Must match global_frame_id in AMCL and global_frame in the global costmap.

description: Search distance along the path to find the closest point to the robot's current pose.
impact: Larger values handle more path deviations but increase CPU. Set to half the robot's typical deviation from the path.

description: Interpolate path curvature beyond the goal point to improve goal approach behavior.
impact: Reduces oscillation when approaching the final goal. Useful when the goal is at a sharp turn.

description: Use the initial_pose_* parameters to set the pose estimate at startup instead of waiting for /initialpose.
impact: Useful in automated deployments where the robot starts at a known pose. Overrides any /initialpose messages.

description: Initial x position estimate in the map frame (used when set_initial_pose is true).
impact: The closer to the robot's true starting position, the faster AMCL converges.

description: Initial y position estimate in the map frame.
impact: The closer to the robot's true starting position, the faster AMCL converges.

description: Initial heading estimate in the map frame.
impact: Wrong initial heading is the most common cause of AMCL failing to converge. Use a compass or fiducial to calibrate.

description: Orient the final path waypoint to match the goal pose's orientation.
impact: When true, the robot arrives at the goal facing the specified direction. Required for docking.

description: List of navigator plugins loaded by the BT navigator.
impact: Requires node restart. Each navigator corresponds to an action server (NavigateToPose, NavigateThroughPoses).

description: Path to the BehaviorTree XML file used by the NavigateToPose action. Empty uses the built-in default tree.
impact: Custom BT files enable custom navigation logic. Wrong path causes startup failure.

description: Enable collision detection in the RPP controller to stop before hitting obstacles.
impact: Safety critical. Disable only for benchmarking. Disabling allows the controller to command paths into obstacles.

description: Maximum time-to-collision allowed when projecting the robot forward to the lookahead carrot point.
impact: Lower values stop the robot earlier before hitting obstacles. Higher values allow passing closer to obstacles.

description: Perform an additional refinement pass after the main smoothing to reduce residual kinks.
impact: Slightly increases computation for visibly cleaner paths. Disable for time-critical planners.

description: Local reference frame used by the behavior server.
impact: Must match the local costmap frame. Changing requires a node restart.

description: Rate at which the behavior server checks collision and updates recovery behavior state.
impact: Higher values improve collision detection during recovery but increase CPU.

description: Topic name for odometry input (used in CLOSED_LOOP feedback mode).
impact: Must match the odometry publisher's topic. Only relevant when feedback=CLOSED_LOOP.

description: Time window for smoothing odometry data in CLOSED_LOOP mode.
impact: Larger values smooth noisy odom but add latency. Smaller values are more responsive.

description: Publish the full costmap on every update instead of only changed cells.
impact: Full publishes are useful for debugging in RViz. In production, partial updates reduce network bandwidth.

description: Publish the full global costmap on every update. Default true because the global map rarely changes.
impact: For large maps, setting to false reduces bandwidth. Ensure clients handle incremental updates.

description: TF transform timestamp tolerance — how old a transform can be and still be accepted.
impact: Increase if TF latency warnings appear (slow systems). Too large and stale transforms cause navigation drift.

description: Maximum age of TF transforms accepted by the local costmap.
impact: Increase on slow hardware or when tf_static latency causes frequent warnings.

description: Maximum age of TF transforms accepted by the global costmap.
impact: Increase on slow hardware. The global costmap updates rarely so this rarely causes issues.

description: Maximum age of TF transforms accepted by AMCL.
impact: AMCL uses a larger tolerance by default because it processes asynchronously. Reduce if localization lags behind true pose.

description: Stateful goal checker remembers that the goal was achieved and does not re-evaluate each tick.
impact: Prevents the goal checker from flickering in/out of success when the robot is at the goal boundary.

description: Positional tolerance for goal achievement — how close the robot must be to the goal XY.
impact: Smaller values require precise stopping. Larger values declare success sooner. Must be > AMCL localization noise.

description: Heading tolerance for goal achievement — how close the robot's heading must be to the goal yaw.
impact: ~14° default. Tighter tolerances improve docking accuracy but may cause spinning at the goal.

description: Minimum distance the robot must travel within the time window to be considered making progress.
impact: Smaller values detect stuck situations faster. Too small and a slow robot in a narrow corridor appears stuck.

description: Time window over which required_movement_radius must be achieved.
impact: Longer windows allow slow passages (tight corridors). Shorter windows cause earlier recovery triggering.

description: KLD sampling: maximum allowed error between the true and estimated distributions.
impact: Smaller values require more particles for the same confidence level. Increase to reduce CPU at the cost of localization accuracy.

description: KLD sampling: upper standard normal quantile for the (1 - delta) confidence interval.
impact: Higher confidence (closer to 1.0) requires more particles. 0.99 = 99% confidence the error is within pf_err.

description: How the obstacle layer combines with layers below it: 0=Overwrite, 1=Max, 2=MaxWithoutUnknownOverwrite.
impact: Max (1) is the standard choice — keeps the highest cost across layers. Overwrite (0) ignores the static map cost under obstacles.

description: How the global obstacle layer combines with layers below it: 0=Overwrite, 1=Max, 2=MaxWithoutUnknownOverwrite.
impact: Max (1) is standard. For the global costmap, MaxWithoutUnknownOverwrite (2) prevents obstacles from overwriting unknown cells, which is useful during exploration.

description: Odometry noise: translation error from rotation and translation (omni model only).
impact: Only used by the OmnidirectionalMotionModel. Ignored for differential drive robots.

description: Re-initialise the particle filter from initial_pose parameters on every restart (not just first run).
impact: Useful in automated testing where the robot always starts at the same pose. Disable in production to preserve localisation across restarts.

description: Enable beam-skip outlier rejection: ignore beams that consistently miss the map.
impact: Improves robustness in environments with glass walls or dynamic obstacles. Requires beam model type.

description: A beam is considered a candidate for skipping if it misses the map by more than this distance.
impact: Tune to the expected thickness of glass walls or transient obstacles in the environment.

description: Fraction of particles that must agree a beam should be skipped before it is actually skipped.
impact: Higher values require stronger consensus before a beam is dismissed. Lower values skip beams more aggressively.

description: If more than this fraction of beams are skipped in a single scan, skip the entire scan update.
impact: Prevents the filter from running on completely unreliable scans (e.g., the robot is in a glass room).

description: Only process the first received map message; ignore subsequent map updates.
impact: Enable if your map server republishes the same map repeatedly. Disable if using a live SLAM map.

description: Exponential decay rate for the short-range sensor model component (beam model only).
impact: Controls how quickly the probability of a short unexpected hit decays with distance. Higher = shorter expected occlusions.

description: Maximum distance to an obstacle in the map for the likelihood field model lookup.
impact: Larger values allow hits further from map obstacles to still contribute. Must be larger than sigma_hit to be meaningful.

description: Topic name on which AMCL subscribes for the occupancy grid map.
impact: Must match the map server's publish topic. Changing requires a node restart.

description: Rate at which AMCL saves the best pose estimate to the parameter server for persistence across restarts.
impact: 0.0 disables pose saving. Useful for robots that restart often and want to resume from last known pose.

description: Topic name on which AMCL subscribes for laser scan data.
impact: Must match the lidar driver's publish topic. Changing requires a node restart.

description: Beam model weight for max-range returns (beam hits at the sensor's maximum range).
impact: Increase if your lidar reports many max-range returns from open areas. Part of the z_hit + z_rand + z_max + z_short = 1.0 constraint.

description: Beam model weight for unexpectedly short returns (caused by unknown obstacles or people).
impact: Increase in environments with many moving obstacles. Part of the z_hit + z_rand + z_max + z_short = 1.0 constraint.

description: Initial z position (unused in 2D navigation; kept for message compatibility).
impact: Always 0.0 for ground robots. Present in the parameter list for 3D pose message compatibility.

description: Odometry topic subscribed to by the controller server for velocity feedback.
impact: Must match the odometry publisher. Changing requires node restart.

description: DWB: maximum linear acceleration in x.
impact: Higher values allow faster speed-ups. Must not exceed the robot's physical motor limits.

description: DWB: maximum lateral acceleration (non-zero for holonomic robots only).
impact: Set to 0 for differential-drive robots. Non-zero on a diff-drive will cause unreachable trajectories.

description: DWB: maximum angular acceleration.
impact: Higher values allow quicker turns. Tune to the robot's wheel torque limits.

description: DWB: maximum linear deceleration in x (negative value).
impact: More negative = harder braking. Set to match physical braking capability. Too aggressive may tip cargo.

description: DWB: maximum lateral deceleration (holonomic only; 0.0 for differential-drive).
impact: Set to 0 for differential-drive robots.

description: DWB: maximum angular deceleration (negative value).
impact: Controls how quickly the robot can stop rotating. Match to motor specs.

description: DWB: maximum forward linear velocity.
impact: Key speed limit. TurtleBot3 max is ~0.22 m/s. Exceeding the robot's hardware limit causes wheel slip.

description: DWB: minimum linear velocity in x. Negative allows reversing.
impact: Set to 0 to prevent reversing on non-reversing robots. Negative enables backward motion.

description: DWB: maximum lateral velocity (holonomic only; 0.0 for differential-drive).
impact: Set to 0 for differential-drive robots. Non-zero for omnidirectional platforms.

description: DWB: minimum lateral velocity (holonomic only; 0.0 for differential-drive).
impact: Set to 0 for differential-drive robots.

description: DWB: maximum angular velocity.
impact: Limits how fast the robot can spin. TurtleBot3 max is ~2.84 rad/s. Higher improves turning agility but may cause localization drift.

description: DWB: minimum translational speed to be sampled. Trajectories slower than this are not considered.
impact: Non-zero prevents very slow creeping motion. Useful on robots that stall at very low speeds.

description: DWB: maximum combined XY speed limit. -1.0 disables the combined limit (x and y are limited independently).
impact: Useful for holonomic robots to limit diagonal speed. -1.0 is standard for differential-drive.

description: DWB: minimum angular speed to be sampled. Trajectories rotating slower than this are not considered.
impact: Non-zero prevents sampling very slow rotations. Increase on robots with angular deadbands.

description: DWB: number of forward velocity samples in the trajectory search space.
impact: More samples give better trajectory selection but increase computation. 20 is a good default for most robots.

description: DWB: number of lateral velocity samples (only relevant for holonomic robots).
impact: Set to 1 for differential-drive (only 0.0 y-velocity is valid). Increase for holonomic robots.

description: DWB: number of angular velocity samples in the trajectory search space.
impact: More samples improve rotation quality. Increase if the robot makes jerky turns.

description: DWB: how far into the future each sampled trajectory is simulated.
impact: Longer simulation catches obstacles further ahead but increases CPU. Short sim_time causes late braking.

description: DWB: angular resolution for trajectory simulation steps.
impact: Smaller values produce smoother simulated trajectories but increase computation. Default is ~1.4°.

description: DWB: ordered list of critic plugin names used to score trajectories.
impact: Requires node restart. Each critic evaluates trajectories on a different criterion. Removing a critic changes navigation behaviour significantly.

description: DWB: remove passed waypoints from the reference path to avoid the robot re-visiting them.
impact: Disable only if the robot needs to track every waypoint of a closed-loop path.

description: DWB: look-back distance along the path when pruning passed waypoints.
impact: Larger values are more forgiving of path deviations before pruning. Too large and the robot backtracks.

description: DWB: stop evaluating a trajectory as soon as any critic marks it invalid.
impact: True significantly reduces computation by skipping further scoring once a trajectory fails. Disable only for debugging critic interactions.

description: DWB: publish the trajectory cost grid as a PointCloud2 for visualisation in RViz.
impact: Useful for debugging why the controller chooses certain paths. Adds CPU and bandwidth overhead; disable in production.

description: DWB BaseObstacle critic: weight for the obstacle avoidance score.
impact: Higher values make the robot more obstacle-averse. Too high dominates all other critics.

description: DWB BaseObstacle critic: sum obstacle scores along the trajectory instead of using the worst.
impact: False = use worst point (safe). True = average over trajectory (may allow passing close to obstacles briefly).

description: DWB GoalAlign critic: weight for aligning the robot's heading with the goal direction.
impact: Higher values strongly prefer trajectories facing the goal. Balance with PathAlign for smooth combined approach.

description: DWB GoalAlign critic: how trajectory pose scores are combined into a single value.
impact: last = only the endpoint matters (standard for goal alignment). sum = all poses contribute equally.

description: DWB GoalAlign critic: distance ahead of each trajectory pose used to compute heading.
impact: Larger values smooth the heading estimate but reduce sensitivity to sharp turns near the goal.

description: DWB GoalDist critic: weight for preferring trajectories that reduce distance to goal.
impact: Higher values strongly drive toward the goal. Balance with PathDist to avoid cutting corners.

description: DWB GoalDist critic: how distance scores are aggregated across the trajectory.
impact: last is standard — only the trajectory endpoint's goal distance matters.

description: DWB PathAlign critic: weight for aligning heading with the global path direction.
impact: Higher values keep the robot pointing along the path. Reduce if the robot oscillates on straight paths.

description: DWB PathAlign critic: aggregation method for heading scores across the trajectory.
impact: last is standard for path alignment. sum makes the entire trajectory prefer path-aligned headings.

description: DWB PathAlign critic: lookahead distance for computing heading alignment with the path.
impact: Larger values smooth heading estimation. Tune with sim_time and robot speed.

description: DWB PathDist critic: weight for staying close to the global path.
impact: The most important tracking critic. Higher values make the robot follow the path more closely at the cost of flexibility around obstacles.

description: DWB PathDist critic: aggregation method for path distance scores.
impact: last measures endpoint's distance to path. sum penalises the full trajectory for drifting.

description: DWB Oscillation critic: weight for penalising oscillatory motion (repeated direction changes).
impact: Increase if the robot rocks back-and-forth. Too high and it avoids all direction changes including legitimate turns.

description: DWB Oscillation critic: distance the robot must travel to reset the oscillation flag.
impact: After reversing, the robot must move this far forward before it can reverse again.

description: DWB Oscillation critic: rotation required to reset the oscillation flag after a direction change.
impact: Prevents the robot from immediately reversing its rotation direction.

description: DWB Oscillation critic: time-based oscillation reset (seconds). -1.0 disables time-based reset.
impact: Non-negative values allow the oscillation flag to clear after the specified time regardless of motion.

description: DWB Oscillation critic: if x velocity is below this and y and theta are above their thresholds, do not mark as oscillating.
impact: Allows the robot to rotate in place without triggering oscillation detection.

description: DWB RotateToGoal critic: weight for the rotate-to-goal phase scoring.
impact: Higher values cause more aggressive in-place rotation when near the goal.

description: DWB RotateToGoal critic: time horizon for the goal approach. -1.0 = use sim_time.
impact: Longer lookahead causes the robot to begin rotating to goal heading earlier.

description: DWB RotateToGoal critic: how much the critic slows the robot when it needs to rotate to reach goal heading.
impact: Higher values cause stronger braking as the robot approaches the goal yaw. Tune to prevent overshooting the goal heading.

description: DWB: distance threshold from goal pose at which the robot is considered to have reached the XY goal.
impact: Smaller values require more precise goal reaching; too small may cause the robot to oscillate near the goal.

description: DWB: translational velocity below which the robot is considered stopped for oscillation detection.
impact: Lower values require the robot to come to a near-complete stop before oscillation logic resets; higher values are more lenient.

description: DWB: if true, velocity commands are clamped to the trajectory limits during trajectory scoring.
impact: Enabling this prevents the commanded velocity from slightly exceeding the scored trajectory velocity.

description: Publish a zero-velocity command when the controller server receives a cancel or reaches the goal.
impact: Ensures the robot stops cleanly. Disabling is rarely needed but can suppress an extra zero-velocity message.

description: DWB: distance ahead of the robot along the global path up to which path points are pruned.
impact: Larger values remove more of the already-passed path; too small and the robot re-evaluates already-visited waypoints.

description: DWB: time step used when forward-simulating trajectories for scoring.
impact: Smaller values improve trajectory scoring accuracy but increase computation. Larger values are faster but less precise.

description: Topic providing the costmap used for collision checking during recovery behaviors.
impact: Must point to the local costmap's raw costmap topic. Changing requires node restart.

description: Topic providing the robot footprint for collision checking during recovery behaviors.
impact: Must match the local costmap's footprint topic. Changing requires node restart.

description: Maximum age of TF transforms accepted by the behavior server.
impact: Increase if TF latency warnings appear during recovery behaviors.

description: Maximum angular velocity used during spin and other rotational recovery behaviors.
impact: Higher values complete rotations faster but may cause localization drift during recovery.

description: Minimum angular velocity during rotational recovery behaviors.
impact: Prevents the robot from rotating too slowly to overcome friction. Increase if the robot stalls mid-spin.

description: Angular acceleration limit during rotational recovery behaviors.
impact: Limits how quickly the robot ramps up rotation speed during recovery. Match to hardware limits.

description: General look-ahead time for collision checking during recovery behaviors.
impact: Longer look-ahead is more conservative (aborts sooner). Shorter allows closer maneuvers.

description: Time step used when simulating robot motion for collision checking.
impact: Smaller steps give more accurate collision detection but increase computation. 0.1s is adequate for most cases.

description: Total projection time for drive_on_heading and backup collision checking.
impact: Longer projection detects obstacles further along the recovery path.

description: C++ plugin class for the spin recovery behavior.
impact: Requires node restart. Change to use a custom spin implementation.

description: C++ plugin class for the backup recovery behavior.
impact: Requires node restart.

description: C++ plugin class for the wait recovery behavior.
impact: Requires node restart.

description: C++ plugin class for the drive-on-heading recovery behavior.
impact: Requires node restart.

description: C++ plugin class for the assisted teleop behavior.
impact: Requires node restart.

description: Acceleration limit for the backup recovery behavior.
impact: Limits how quickly the robot ramps up speed while backing up.

description: Deceleration limit for the backup recovery behavior (negative value).
impact: Controls stopping distance when ending a backup maneuver.

description: Minimum speed during a backup maneuver to prevent stalling.
impact: Non-zero prevents the robot from stopping mid-backup on robots with low-speed deadbands.

description: Acceleration limit for the drive-on-heading behavior.
impact: Limits ramp-up speed during forward recovery motion.

description: Deceleration limit for the drive-on-heading behavior (negative value).
impact: Controls stopping distance at the end of a drive-on-heading maneuver.

description: Path to the BehaviorTree XML for the NavigateToPose action. Empty string uses the built-in default tree.
impact: Custom BTs enable sophisticated navigation logic. Wrong path causes startup failure.

description: Path to the BehaviorTree XML for the NavigateThroughPoses action.
impact: Custom BTs enable multi-waypoint navigation logic. Empty uses the built-in default.

description: Odometry topic used by the BT navigator for velocity-based decisions.
impact: Must match the odometry publisher. Changing requires node restart.

description: Maximum age of TF transforms accepted by the BT navigator.
impact: Increase if TF latency warnings appear. Too large causes stale pose estimates.

description: Reload the BehaviorTree XML from disk on every navigation request instead of caching it.
impact: Enable during BT development to pick up XML changes without restarting the node. Disable in production.

description: Name of the BT blackboard key used to pass the navigation goal pose.
impact: Must match the key name used in your custom BT XML files.

description: Name of the BT blackboard key used to pass the list of goals for NavigateThroughPoses.
impact: Must match the key name in your BT XML. Only relevant for navigate_through_poses action.

description: Name of the BT blackboard key used to share the planned path between BT nodes.
impact: Must match the key name used in your BT XML for path sharing.

description: List of BehaviorTree plugin libraries to load. Each library provides BT condition and action nodes.
impact: Requires node restart. Removing a library that a BT XML references causes startup failure.

description: Topic providing the costmap used by the smoother for obstacle-aware path smoothing.
impact: Must point to a valid costmap raw topic. Changing requires node restart.

description: Topic providing the robot footprint for collision checking during path smoothing.
impact: Must match the costmap's footprint topic. Changing requires node restart.

description: Robot base frame used by the smoother for pose lookups.
impact: Must match URDF base link and other Nav2 nodes. Changing requires node restart.

description: Maximum age of TF transforms accepted by the smoother server.
impact: Increase if TF latency warnings appear during path smoothing.

description: C++ plugin class for the simple path smoother.
impact: Requires node restart. Change to use a custom smoother implementation.

description: C++ plugin class for the wait-at-waypoint task executor.
impact: Requires node restart. Change to use a custom waypoint task (e.g. take a photo, charge).

description: Enable or disable the wait-at-waypoint task executor.
impact: Disable to skip the pause at each waypoint and proceed immediately.

description: C++ plugin class for the 3D voxel obstacle layer.
impact: Requires costmap restart. Change to use a custom obstacle layer implementation.

description: Space-separated list of sensor source names used by the voxel layer.
impact: Requires costmap restart. Each source name must have a corresponding set of .topic, .data_type, etc. parameters.

description: Publish the 3D voxel grid as a VoxelGrid message for visualisation in RViz.
impact: Useful for debugging sensor coverage. Adds bandwidth overhead; disable in production.

description: Maximum height above the floor at which obstacles are inserted into the voxel layer.
impact: Set to slightly above the robot's height to ignore overhead beams. Too low and tall obstacles are missed.

description: Minimum height at which obstacles are inserted into the voxel layer.
impact: Increase to ignore ground-level returns (e.g., carpet, floor reflections).

description: Sensor topic subscribed to by the voxel layer 'scan' source.
impact: Must match the lidar driver's publish topic. Changing requires costmap restart.

description: Message type for the voxel layer 'scan' sensor source.
impact: Use LaserScan for 2D lidars, PointCloud2 for 3D lidars or depth cameras.

description: Allow the voxel layer 'scan' source to mark (insert) obstacles.
impact: Disable to use this sensor for clearing only (e.g. a downward-facing sensor that shouldn't add obstacles).

description: Allow the voxel layer 'scan' source to clear (ray-trace free space) through the costmap.
impact: Disable to keep old obstacles even when the sensor line-of-sight passes through them.

description: Maximum range at which the voxel layer 'scan' source inserts obstacles.
impact: Larger values populate the costmap with more distant obstacles. Too large causes noise from far reflections.

description: Maximum range for ray-tracing free space through the voxel layer 'scan' source.
impact: Longer ray traces remove stale obstacles faster but trace through more cells per update.

description: Voxel layer: points above this height are ignored as obstacles.
impact: Set to robot or environment ceiling height to avoid false positives from overhead structures.

description: Voxel layer: points below this height are ignored as obstacles (e.g. floor reflections).
impact: Raise this to suppress ground reflections or low-lying noise; set too high and low obstacles are missed.

description: Voxel layer: scan returns closer than this range are ignored as obstacles.
impact: Increase to suppress near-body sensor noise. Too high and nearby obstacles are missed.

description: Voxel layer: raytrace clearing does not clear cells closer than this range.
impact: Prevents the sensor body from clearing voxels immediately around the robot due to near-range returns.

description: Width of the global costmap in cells. 0 = determined by the loaded map.
impact: Normally 0 so the costmap exactly matches the map server's map dimensions.

description: Height of the global costmap in cells. 0 = determined by the loaded map.
impact: Normally 0 so the costmap exactly matches the map server's map dimensions.

description: Keep the global costmap centered on the robot (rolling window). Should be false for global planning.
impact: False is standard for the global costmap — it covers the full map. True is for the local costmap only.

description: Circular approximation of the robot radius for the global costmap collision checks.
impact: Set to the robot's actual radius. Larger values add safety margin but restrict narrow-passage planning.

description: Enable the global costmap obstacle layer for dynamic obstacle insertion.
impact: Disabling removes dynamic obstacles from global planning. The static layer still provides the base map.

description: Space-separated sensor source names for the global costmap obstacle layer.
impact: Requires costmap restart. Each name maps to a set of .topic, .data_type, .obstacle_max_range parameters.

description: Sensor topic subscribed to by the global costmap obstacle layer 'scan' source.
impact: Must match the lidar driver's topic. Changing requires costmap restart.

description: Message type for the global costmap obstacle layer 'scan' source.
impact: Use LaserScan for 2D lidars, PointCloud2 for 3D sensors.

description: Maximum range for obstacle insertion into the global costmap obstacle layer.
impact: Larger values add more distant obstacles to the global plan. Can cause plan avoidance of far transient objects.

description: Maximum range for ray-tracing free space through the global costmap obstacle layer.
impact: Longer traces clear more space on the global costmap as the robot moves, removing stale dynamic obstacles.

description: Topic name for incoming teleop velocity commands used by AssistedTeleop behavior.
impact: Must match the topic published by your teleop node (e.g. joy_node or keyboard_teleop).

description: Minimum linear speed the DriveOnHeading behavior will command, even when slowing to stop.
impact: Setting above 0 prevents the robot from crawling at very low speeds on approach.

description: Plugin class name for the BaseObstacle DWB critic. Identifies the C++ class the plugin system loads.
impact: Changing this to an unregistered class will prevent the controller from starting.

description: Plugin class name for the GoalAlign DWB critic.
impact: Changing this to an unregistered class will prevent the controller from starting.

description: Plugin class name for the GoalDist DWB critic.
impact: Changing this to an unregistered class will prevent the controller from starting.

description: Plugin class name for the Oscillation DWB critic.
impact: Changing this to an unregistered class will prevent the controller from starting.

description: Plugin class name for the PathAlign DWB critic.
impact: Changing this to an unregistered class will prevent the controller from starting.

description: Plugin class name for the PathDist DWB critic.
impact: Changing this to an unregistered class will prevent the controller from starting.

description: Plugin class name for the RotateToGoal DWB critic.
impact: Changing this to an unregistered class will prevent the controller from starting.

description: Publish detailed per-trajectory debug info (critic scores per trajectory).
impact: Disable to reduce publish overhead; useful in high-frequency production deployments.

description: List of C++ namespaces to search when resolving critic plugin class names.
impact: Add your plugin's namespace if using custom critics not in the default dwb_critics namespace.

description: If true, discretize trajectory by equal time steps; if false, by equal distance steps.
impact: Time-based discretization can produce smoother trajectories at varying speeds.

description: Whether to include the final point of a simulated trajectory in critic scoring.
impact: Including the last point penalizes trajectories that overshoot the goal.

description: Step size along the trajectory used for obstacle checking (linear interpolation).
impact: Smaller values give finer collision checking but increase CPU load.

description: Lifetime of RViz visualization markers published by DWB (trajectory candidates, scores).
impact: Lower values clear markers faster; 0 means markers persist until overwritten.

description: Publish the DWBLocalPlannerEvaluation message containing all trajectory scores.
impact: Disable to reduce topic bandwidth when not debugging the planner.

description: Re-publish the global plan received from the planner server.
impact: Disable to reduce bandwidth; the plan is already published by planner_server.

description: Publish the selected (best) local trajectory as a path for RViz visualization.
impact: Disable to reduce bandwidth when not visualizing local planning.

description: Publish all sampled trajectory candidates as a MarkerArray for RViz debugging.
impact: Disable in production to eliminate significant publish overhead.

description: Publish the global plan transformed into the local costmap frame.
impact: Useful for debugging misaligned global/local frames; disable when not needed.

description: Trim the transformed global plan to only include points within the local costmap bounds.
impact: Keep enabled to avoid sending the controller unnecessary far-future waypoints.

description: Fully-qualified C++ class name of the trajectory generator plugin used by DWB.
impact: Change to dwb_plugins::LimitedAccelGenerator to enforce kinodynamic constraints on sampled trajectories.

description: Plugin class for the general goal checker. Determines when the robot has reached its goal.
impact: Replace with a custom checker to implement custom goal-reached logic.

description: Plugin class for the progress checker. Monitors whether the robot is making navigation progress.
impact: Replace with a custom checker for alternative stuck-detection strategies.

description: Topic name on which the controller listens for SpeedLimit messages to dynamically cap velocity.
impact: Publish a nav2_msgs/SpeedLimit message to this topic to reduce max speed (e.g. near humans).

description: List of costmap filter plugin names to load (e.g. keepout zones, speed limit zones).
impact: Add filter plugin names here to activate costmap filtering behaviors.

description: Robot footprint polygon as a list of [x,y] points (meters). Overrides robot_radius if set.
impact: A polygon footprint gives more accurate collision checking for non-circular robots.

description: Extra padding added around the robot footprint for costmap inflation calculations.
impact: Larger padding gives more conservative obstacle avoidance; smaller allows tighter passages.

description: Enable or disable the inflation layer in the global costmap.
impact: Disabling removes cost gradient around obstacles — the robot may plan paths too close to walls.

description: Whether to inflate costs around unknown (unexplored) cells in the global costmap.
impact: Enable to treat unexplored areas conservatively; disable to allow planning into unknown space.

description: If true, unknown cells are treated as obstacles for inflation purposes.
impact: Inflating unknown cells prevents paths through unmapped areas at the cost of reduced exploration.

description: C++ plugin class for the inflation layer.
impact: Changing to a different class replaces the inflation algorithm entirely.

description: Costmap cost value at or above which a cell is considered an impassable obstacle.
impact: Lower values make the planner treat more cells as lethal; raising it allows paths through high-cost areas.

description: Topic name for the static map used by the global costmap.
impact: Change if your map server publishes on a non-default topic or you use a namespaced map.

description: Space-separated list of sensor observation source names for the obstacle layer.
impact: Each name must have a matching set of observation_source parameters defined.

description: Clear obstacle layer cells within the robot footprint as the robot moves.
impact: Disable if ghost obstacles persist at the robot's current position.

description: Maximum height of sensor readings to consider as obstacles in the obstacle layer.
impact: Lower this to ignore tall ceilings or overhead structures in 3D sensor data.

description: Minimum height of sensor readings to consider as obstacles.
impact: Raise to ignore floor-level noise from 3D sensors.

description: C++ plugin class for the obstacle layer.
impact: Swap for VoxelLayer or a custom plugin to change obstacle representation.

description: Whether this sensor source is used to clear free-space in the obstacle layer.
impact: Disable to stop free-space clearing from this sensor (useful for marking-only sensors).

description: Expected publish rate of the sensor. Used to detect stale data. 0 disables staleness checking.
impact: Set to your sensor's actual rate to get warnings when the sensor stops publishing.

description: Treat infinite range returns from the laser as a valid max-range reading rather than invalid.
impact: Enable for lidars that return inf for out-of-range measurements (e.g. some RPLidars).

description: Whether this sensor source marks obstacles in the costmap.
impact: Disable to use this sensor only for clearing without marking new obstacles.

description: Maximum obstacle height for this specific sensor source.
impact: Per-source override of obstacle_layer.max_obstacle_height for heterogeneous sensor setups.

description: Minimum obstacle height for this specific sensor source.
impact: Per-source override; raise to ignore floor returns from tilted 3D sensors.

description: How long obstacle observations from this sensor persist in the costmap. 0 = keep until new data arrives.
impact: Increase for slow sensors so obstacles don't disappear between scans.

description: Minimum sensor range at which readings are used to mark obstacles.
impact: Raise to ignore very close returns that may be the robot's own body.

description: Minimum range for raytrace (free-space clearing) operations.
impact: Raise to prevent clearing cells very close to the sensor origin.

description: TF frame of the sensor. If empty, the frame_id from the incoming message is used.
impact: Set explicitly if the message header frame_id is wrong or remapped.

description: X-coordinate of the costmap's bottom-left corner in the global frame.
impact: For rolling window costmaps this is updated automatically; set for fixed-size costmaps.

description: Y-coordinate of the costmap's bottom-left corner in the global frame.
impact: For rolling window costmaps this is updated automatically; set for fixed-size costmaps.

description: Clear static map cells inside the robot footprint as the robot moves.
impact: Enable only if the static map has errors at the robot's starting position.

description: Override topic for the static layer's map subscription (overrides global map_topic if set).
impact: Use to subscribe to a different map topic than the one used by the costmap master.

description: C++ plugin class for the static layer.
impact: Swap for a custom static layer plugin to change how the occupancy grid is processed.

description: Subscribe to incremental map update messages in addition to full map messages.
impact: Enable to receive partial OccupancyGrid updates; disable for static, non-changing maps.

description: Per-layer TF transform tolerance for the static layer (overrides global transform_tolerance if > 0).
impact: Set when the static layer needs a different TF staleness tolerance than other layers.

description: Maintain a separate representation for unknown space rather than treating it as free.
impact: True gives planner awareness of unexplored areas. False treats all non-obstacle cells as free.

description: Use a three-value (free/unknown/occupied) costmap representation from the static layer.
impact: Disable to allow continuous cost values from the static map rather than snapping to free/occupied.

description: Cost value used to represent unknown cells in the costmap (255 = standard ROS unknown).
impact: Changing this affects how planners interpret unexplored space.

description: When combining costmap layers, use the maximum cost value instead of the default combination method.
impact: Enabling uses the highest cost from all layers; default combines them additively.

description: List of costmap filter plugin names to load for the local costmap (e.g. speed limit zones).
impact: Add filter plugin names here to activate local costmap filtering.

description: Extra padding added around the robot footprint in the local costmap.
impact: Larger values give more conservative local obstacle avoidance.

description: Enable or disable the inflation layer in the local costmap.
impact: Disabling removes cost gradient — the controller may plan trajectories too close to obstacles.

description: Inflate costs around unknown cells in the local costmap.
impact: Enable to treat unexplored cells conservatively in local planning.

description: Treat unknown cells as obstacles for inflation in the local costmap.
impact: Inflating unknown cells prevents the controller from commanding the robot into unmapped areas.

description: C++ plugin class for the local costmap inflation layer.
impact: Changing replaces the inflation algorithm for local planning.

description: Cost value at or above which a local costmap cell is considered an impassable obstacle.
impact: Lower values make the controller more conservative; higher allows navigation near obstacles.

description: Topic for a static map layer within the local costmap (if a static layer is configured).
impact: Typically unused in rolling-window local costmaps without a static layer.

description: Space-separated list of sensor observation source names for the local costmap obstacle layer.
impact: Each name must have matching observation_source parameters.

description: X origin of the local costmap. For rolling window maps, updated automatically as robot moves.
impact: Generally not set manually for rolling-window costmaps.

description: Y origin of the local costmap. For rolling window maps, updated automatically as robot moves.
impact: Generally not set manually for rolling-window costmaps.

description: Maintain a separate representation for unknown space in the local costmap.
impact: Typically false for local costmap (rolling window) — all unseen cells treated as free.

description: Use three-value (free/unknown/occupied) representation from the static layer in local costmap.
impact: Typically true even for local costmaps with a static layer component.

description: Cost value representing unknown cells in the local costmap.
impact: Matches the standard ROS unknown occupancy cost (255).

description: Combine local costmap layers using maximum cost rather than the default method.
impact: Enable to use the highest cost from all layers at each cell.

description: Method for combining voxel layer costs with other layers. 0=override, 1=max, 2=additive.
impact: Controls how voxel obstacles interact with inflation and other layer costs.

description: Clear voxel layer cells inside the robot footprint as the robot moves.
impact: Prevents the robot from seeing itself as an obstacle in the voxel grid.

description: Minimum number of voxels in a column required to mark a 2D costmap cell as occupied.
impact: Increase to ignore single-point noise; decrease to detect lower-profile obstacles.

description: Z origin of the voxel grid (height above the ground plane where voxels start).
impact: Adjust if the voxel grid should exclude ground-level returns.

description: Expected publish rate of the voxel layer sensor. 0 disables staleness checking.
impact: Set to sensor rate to detect when sensor data stops arriving.

description: Treat infinite range readings from the voxel layer sensor as valid max-range measurements.
impact: Enable for sensors that return inf instead of 0 for out-of-range readings.

description: How long 3D voxel observations persist. 0 = until next observation from this source.
impact: Increase for slow 3D sensors to prevent obstacles disappearing between scans.

description: TF frame for the voxel layer sensor. Empty uses the message header frame_id.
impact: Override if the published sensor frame does not match the TF tree.

description: Maximum number of unknown voxels in a column for the cell to still be marked as unknown (vs free).
impact: Higher values keep more cells unknown; lower values clear unknown cells to free more aggressively.

description: Enable or disable the collision monitor at startup. When false the node activates but does not act on incoming velocity commands.
impact: Set to false to temporarily bypass all collision safety zones without stopping the node.

description: TF frame attached to the robot base used as the reference frame for all polygon and source transformations.
impact: Must match the robot's TF tree. Wrong value causes transform failures and disables all collision detection.

description: Odometry TF frame used to project the robot's future trajectory when evaluating approach-type polygons.
impact: Must match the odometry frame in the TF tree. Incorrect value causes trajectory projection to fail for approach action types.

description: Maximum age of a TF transform before it is considered too old to use. Applies to all source and polygon frame lookups.
impact: Increase on systems with high TF latency or slow sensors. Too large a value may use stale poses and produce incorrect collision estimates.

description: Maximum time a sensor source may go without publishing before it is considered invalid and ignored. 0 disables timeout checking.
impact: Lower values make the system more sensitive to sensor dropouts (safer but noisier). Higher values tolerate slow sensors but may miss obstacle data during outages.

description: Duration for which a zero-velocity (stop) command continues to be published after the triggering condition clears. Prevents the robot from immediately resuming motion.
impact: Increase for conservative behaviour in cluttered environments. Decrease to allow faster recovery when obstacles clear.

description: Topic on which the collision monitor subscribes to incoming velocity commands from the controller or smoother.
impact: Must be remapped to match the upstream velocity publisher. Mismatch results in no commands being received and the robot not moving.

description: Topic on which the collision monitor publishes the (potentially modified or zeroed) velocity command to the robot hardware interface.
impact: Must match the topic the robot's hardware driver subscribes to. Mismatch means safety-limited commands never reach the robot.

description: List of named polygon (or circle) zone identifiers that the collision monitor will configure and evaluate. Each entry must have a corresponding set of <name>.type, <name>.action_type, and shape parameters.
impact: Defines which safety zones are active. Adding zones increases CPU usage slightly. Omitting this parameter causes the node to fail on startup.

description: List of named sensor source identifiers (e.g. laser scans, point clouds) that feed obstacle data into the collision monitor. Each entry must have a corresponding set of <name>.type and <name>.topic parameters.
impact: Determines which sensors are used for collision detection. Missing or mismatched names cause sources to be silently ignored. Omitting this parameter causes the node to fail on startup.

description: Topic on which the collision monitor publishes its current state (active action type and triggering polygon). Empty string disables state publishing.
impact: Enable to monitor which safety zone is currently active for debugging or higher-level behaviour trees. Has no effect on collision-avoidance behaviour itself.

description: When true, sensor data is shifted to account for robot base motion between the time the scan was captured and the time it is processed, improving accuracy at higher speeds.
impact: Disable only on very slow robots or when transform latency causes overcorrection. Leaving it enabled is the safe default; disabling may cause phantom or missed detections at speed.

description: Safety action taken when the polygon zone is triggered. Accepted values: 'stop' (zero velocity), 'slowdown' (scale velocity by slowdown_ratio), 'limit' (cap linear/angular velocity), 'approach' (project trajectory and stop before collision), 'none' (no action, monitoring only).
impact: Determines how aggressively the robot reacts to objects in the zone. 'stop' is safest but most disruptive. 'approach' allows smooth deceleration but requires accurate odometry. Required — omitting this parameter causes node startup to fail.

description: Minimum number of sensor data points that must fall inside the polygon zone to trigger the configured action. Helps reject spurious single-point detections.
impact: Increase to reduce false positives from sensor noise. Decrease for faster reaction to sparse obstacles (e.g. thin poles). A value of 1 triggers on any single point inside the zone.

description: When true, publishes a PolygonStamped marker on the topic '<polygon_name>' (configurable via polygon_pub_topic) so the zone can be visualised in RViz.
impact: Enable during development and tuning to confirm zone shapes are correct. Adds a small topic-publishing overhead; disable in production if not needed.

description: Topic name for this observation source's sensor data. Each observation source (laser scan, pointcloud, range, etc.) subscribes to this topic to receive its sensor readings.
impact: Must match the topic published by the corresponding sensor driver. An incorrect topic causes the source to receive no data and silently contribute nothing to collision detection.

description: Minimum z-height of PointCloud2 points (in the global frame) to be considered as obstacles. Points below this threshold are discarded.
impact: Lower values include near-floor points and may cause false positives from floor reflections. Raise to filter out ground plane noise; lower if the sensor is mounted high and low obstacles must be detected.

description: Maximum z-height of PointCloud2 points (in the global frame) to be considered as obstacles. Points above this threshold are discarded.
impact: Increase to detect taller obstacles such as walls, people, and furniture. Decrease to ignore overhead structure like ceiling-mounted equipment or tree canopies.

description: Time horizon used by the APPROACH polygon action. The robot's future trajectory is simulated this many seconds ahead; if the footprint would intersect the polygon within that window, velocity is reduced. Only used when action_type is 'approach'.
impact: Larger value triggers slowdown earlier and further from the obstacle, giving more stopping margin. Smaller value allows the robot to approach closer before decelerating, which can reduce navigation hesitation.

description: Time step used when numerically integrating robot motion during the APPROACH polygon collision prediction. At each step the footprint is projected forward and tested against the polygon. Only used when action_type is 'approach'.
impact: Smaller step improves collision detection accuracy along curved paths but increases CPU usage per control cycle. The default of 0.1 s is appropriate for most indoor robots.

description: Velocity scaling factor applied when the robot enters a SLOWDOWN polygon zone. The incoming velocity command is multiplied by this ratio. Only used when action_type is 'slowdown'.
impact: Lower value slows the robot more aggressively near obstacles. A value of 1.0 effectively disables the slowdown. Tune alongside zone size to achieve the desired approach behaviour.

description: Topic providing the robot footprint geometry used for APPROACH polygon collision prediction. The footprint is projected along the simulated trajectory to check for intersections with the zone. Only used when action_type is 'approach'.
impact: Must match the footprint topic published by the costmap (or any other publisher). An incorrect topic causes the approach calculation to use a zero or stale footprint, leading to incorrect collision predictions.

description: List of directories to search for Behavior Tree XML files. At startup each directory is scanned for .xml files so they can be referenced by filename in the default_nav_to_pose_bt_xml / default_nav_through_poses_bt_xml parameters or passed as a goal.
impact: Add custom BT directories here to make your own trees available without specifying full paths. The package-share path is always searched by default.

description: Maximum time the BT action server will wait for a cancel request to be acknowledged and the tree to reach a terminal state before returning CANCELED.
impact: Too small a value may cause the navigator to report failure on cancel before the BT has finished cleaning up. Increase if plugins need extra time to abort mid-action.

description: List of action-name prefixes used to harvest error codes from the BT blackboard after execution. Each prefix is combined with '_error_code' to form the blackboard key read at the end of a BT tick.
impact: Must include a prefix for every action server whose error codes should propagate to the navigator result. Missing entries cause error codes to be silently dropped and result in unhelpful UNKNOWN failure reasons.

description: Time window for the OdomSmoother used to compute the robot's current speed from the odometry topic. The smoother averages odometry messages received within this window.
impact: Larger values produce a smoother speed estimate but add lag, which affects estimated-time-to-goal feedback accuracy. 0.3 s is a good default for typical odometry rates.

description: TCP port used by the Groot2 monitoring server for the navigator. Declared per-navigator sub-namespace (e.g. navigate_to_pose.groot_server_port). Both navigate_to_pose and navigate_through_poses default to 1669.
impact: Only relevant when enable_groot_monitoring is true. Change if the port conflicts with another service. Both navigators share the same default so they must be given different ports when run simultaneously.

description: Controls ROS 2 service-call introspection for the navigator's action server. Valid values: 'disabled' (off), 'metadata' (record request/response metadata only), 'contents' (record full request/response data). Requires rclcpp >= 29.
impact: Enabling introspection is useful for debugging action call flow but adds overhead. Use 'metadata' for lightweight tracing and 'contents' only during focused debugging sessions.

description: Maximum path length (meters) searched forward along the global path from the last known closest segment when computing distance-to-goal feedback. Declared per-navigator sub-namespace (e.g. navigate_to_pose.search_window).
impact: A larger window ensures the closest point is found even after large odometry jumps, at slightly higher CPU cost. Too small a value may lock onto a stale segment, causing incorrect distance-remaining estimates.

description: Time step used when propagating sampled trajectories in the MPPI optimizer. Must equal the controller period (1/controller_frequency); MPPI will raise an error if they differ.
impact: Shorter steps improve trajectory accuracy but increase per-step computation. A value that does not match the controller period will cause MPPI to refuse to start.

description: Number of time steps in each sampled trajectory. Horizon length = time_steps * model_dt (e.g. 56 * 0.05 s = 2.8 s look-ahead).
impact: More steps extend the planning horizon, improving anticipation of distant obstacles and curves at the cost of higher memory and compute per control cycle. Too few steps causes the optimizer to react too late.

description: Number of random trajectory samples evaluated per optimization step. The nav2_bringup default YAML uses 2000.
impact: Larger batches improve solution quality and robustness in cluttered environments at the cost of proportionally higher CPU/GPU load. Reduce on resource-constrained hardware.

description: Number of MPPI optimization iterations performed per control cycle. Each iteration re-samples noise around the previous best control sequence.
impact: More iterations refine the control solution and can improve tracking in tight spaces, but multiply computation time proportionally. 1 is sufficient for most environments.

description: Softmax temperature lambda used in the MPPI importance-sampling weight: w_k = exp(-cost_k / temperature). Controls how sharply the optimizer focuses on low-cost trajectories.
impact: Lower values make the controller more aggressive by concentrating weight on the very best trajectories; higher values average over more samples, giving smoother but potentially sub-optimal commands.

description: Discount factor applied to the control-effort regularization term in the MPPI cost. Scales how much deviation of sampled controls from the nominal sequence is penalized.
impact: Increasing gamma penalizes control sequences that deviate strongly from the previous solution, producing smoother command trajectories. Too high a value can restrict the optimizer from finding better paths.

description: Pluggable kinematic motion model used to propagate sampled trajectories. "diff_drive" (non-holonomic), "omni" (holonomic with independent vy), or "ackermann" (car-like with minimum turning radius).
impact: Must match the robot drive type. A mismatch causes trajectories to be kinematically infeasible, degrading path tracking. Requires a matching motion model plugin to be loaded.
